title: fix(collection): prevent markup parsing
body:
Prevent the request description trying to parse the content with markup.

Fixes #241.

step_plan.summary: Wrap the request.description in a rich.text.Text renderable inside RequestPreview.watch_request so the collection preview shows the literal description text and Textual/Rich does not parse user-provided markup-like sequences.

steps.operation: Update RequestPreview.watch_request to pass a rich.Text renderable to Static.update
steps.reason: Textual/Rich parses markup in raw strings passed to Static, causing user-provided descriptions containing markup-like sequences (e.g., "[b]...") to be interpreted as formatting. Wrapping the description in rich.text.Text (e.g., Text(request.description or "")) produces a plain-text renderable that prevents markup parsing and ensures the description is shown literally.
steps.side_effects: Presentation-only change: preview will render descriptions literally instead of interpreting markup. Add an import for rich.text.Text at the top of the file; ensure linters/formatters accept the new import. Snapshot/UI tests that previously relied on markup parsing in the preview may need updating. No API or data-model changes; other Static usages that intentionally rely on markup remain unchanged because they still pass strings. Minimal risk to other code paths, but run unit and UI/component tests (and update snapshots) to catch regressions in preview rendering.
